examples.title: Linear Search Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates linear search with O(n) time complexity
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LinearSearch(arr, n, key)
BEGIN
    FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
        IF arr[i] == key THEN
            RETURN i  // Element found at index i
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN -1  // Element not found
END
examples.explanation: Linear search sequentially checks each element until target is found or list ends. Works on both sorted and unsorted arrays.

examples.title: Binary Search Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates binary search with O(log n) time complexity on sorted array
examples.explanation: Binary search repeatedly divides the sorted array in half, comparing middle element with target. Requires sorted array.

examples.title: Array Insertion Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates insertion in array with O(n) worst case complexity
examples.explanation: Insertion at a specific position requires shifting all elements after that position to make room. Most efficient at end O(1), least efficient at beginning O(n).

examples.title: Stack Operations using Array
examples.description: Demonstrates Stack implementation with push and pop operations
examples.explanation: Stack follows LIFO principle. Push adds element to top, Pop removes from top. Both operations are O(1) in this array implementation.
